hypothesis: Unreferenced tools >20, periodic fulfillment >70%, orient wiring <60%
method: Enumerate all tools/*.py files. Check each against 6 wiring files (orient.py, maintenance.py, periodics.json, check.sh, CLAUDE.md, SWARM.md). Categorize as wired/referenced/unreferenced. Compute periodic fulfillment rate from periodics.json cadence vs last-run dates.
expect: Unreferenced tools >20 (L-899 baseline 24). Periodic fulfillment >70%. Orient wiring <60%.
actual: Unreferenced 25 (32.5%). Periodic fulfillment 70.8%. Wired 66.2% (orient+maintenance are the two hub tools at 6/6 density).
diff: Unreferenced count matched (25 vs >20 expected). Periodic fulfillment barely met threshold (70.8% vs >70%). Wiring coverage WRONG — 66.2% not <60% (more tools wired than expected). Novel: wiring density is bimodal — tools are either deeply wired (4-6/6) or completely unwired (0/6). Mean 1.71 hides this distribution.
conclusion: F-META17 baseline established. Three actionable findings: (1) 25 unreferenced tools (32.5%) — some are genuinely dead (genesis_selector, swarm_profiler), others are actively useful but unwired (citation_retrieval, frontier_triage, eval_sufficiency). Triage needed. (2) 7 overdue periodics — signal-audit (21s) and fundamental-setup-reswarm (11s) are worst. (3) Bimodal wiring: tools either get deeply integrated or remain islands. The 2 indirectly-wired tools (domain_map, genesis_evolve) show that import chains create shadow wiring not captured by string search.
